releases.title: collectZ v3.18.4
releases.summary: This patch replaces frontend helper and state names that used private deployment language with neutral local-runtime wording. It keeps the existing backend contract value intact, but makes the frontend source less tied to private product labels.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Renamed frontend runtime helper names away from private deployment terminology.
Renamed navigation and dashboard state variables to use `localRuntime` wording.
Preserved the existing backend `product_edition` contract behavior for compatibility.
Reduced the public-source boundary audit from `27` findings across `5` files to `12` findings across `3` files.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.3
releases.summary: This patch removes the remaining `control plane` wording from the candidate public source surfaces. It is one step in making the public mirror useful enough to include frontend source and API contract files later.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Reworded the admin workspace management description in the frontend.
Reworded four OpenAPI admin workspace summaries away from internal operations language.
Reduced the public-source boundary audit from `32` findings across `6` files to `27` findings across `5` files.
Confirmed the `control plane` blocker category is cleared from the audit.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.2
releases.summary: This release adds the first explicit public-source boundary audit. The public mirror remains an install/support mirror for now, but maintainers can now measure exactly which frontend and OpenAPI source surfaces still block a useful source mirror.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `npm run audit:public-source-boundary` to scan candidate source-publication surfaces.
Added a JSON audit report under ignored local artifacts so blockers can be reviewed without committing evidence files.
Tracked blocker categories for private runtime language, runtime contract names, operations wording, auth/test bypass labels, and private docs references.
Updated public mirror docs and sanitizer strategy to make clear that application source remains private until this boundary is intentionally cleaned.
Tightened the public export contract so source folders stay denied until the audit findings are resolved.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.1
releases.summary: This release adds the local public mirror export builder for the sanitization strategy introduced in `3.18.0`. It can generate a reviewed public tree and optional clean local commit without pushing or publishing anything automatically.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `npm run public:export` for building a sanitized local public export tree from `public-export.manifest.json`.
Added optional `--commit` support to initialize a separate git repository inside the export tree and create a clean public export commit.
Added local-gate enforcement so normal exports require a current passing `npm run release:local-gate` report.
Added export-tree validation for denied paths, symlinks, setup-surface content patterns, and secret-like public setup values.
Updated the public README language so the generated mirror does not depend on maintainer-only `docs/wiki` paths.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.0
releases.summary: This release defines the public repository sanitization strategy for collectZ: a private source-of-truth repository can generate a clean public mirror without exposing maintainer workflow, release evidence, roadmap/backlog detail, local overrides, or private git history.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `public-export.manifest.json` as the first machine-readable public mirror contract.
Extended `npm run validate:public-export` so it requires the manifest, clean public history policy, required denylists, and local-gate publication expectation.
Added maintainer documentation for the private-source/public-mirror operating model, public mirror contents, publication workflow, public checks stance, and accidental leak response.
Promoted the public repository sanitization backlog item into the `3.18.0` roadmap slice.
Kept actual mirror publication and clean export commit automation as a follow-up.

releases.title: collectZ v3.17.0
releases.summary: This release adds the first local CI/CD gate for collectZ maintainers, giving pre-push work one standard command that checks release, test, dependency, and evidence readiness before public CI runs.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `npm run release:local-gate` for the standard local pre-push validation profile.

Added `npm run release:local-gate:full` for heavier local checks such as CodeQL, secret scanning, runtime smoke, browser regression, and image/SBOM readiness when local tools and runtime state are available.
Added `npm run release:install-hooks` to install an opt-in pre-push hook that runs the standard local gate.
Added local gate reports under `artifacts/local-ci/` with redaction for common secret-bearing output patterns.
Documented the local CI/CD workflow, blocked-gate semantics, hook bypass expectation, and relationship to hosted CI.
Promoted the local CI/CD backlog item into the roadmap as the first `3.17.0` infrastructure slice.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.36
releases.summary: This patch completes the maintained-source CodeQL baseline by dispositioning the reviewed Sched ICS request-forgery boundary while keeping the runtime URL guardrails in source.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a `query-filters` disposition for `js/request-forgery` alongside the existing `js/http-to-file-access` exclusion in `.github/codeql/codeql-config.yml`.
Kept the maintained-source suite and generated-output path ignores in place.
Added a source-local `// codeql[js/request-forgery]` suppression on the reviewed Sched ICS fetch sink so local maintained-source analysis stays aligned with the runtime URL boundary.
Updated the maintainer-facing CodeQL contract test so the workflow/config documentation now includes the reviewed request-forgery disposition.
Synced version metadata for the root, backend, and frontend app manifests.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.35
releases.summary: This patch keeps maintained-source CodeQL focused on actionable source findings by moving the authoritative query selection into a checked-in suite and dispositioning the intentional HTTP-to-file upload/artifact pattern.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `.github/codeql/collectz-maintained-source.qls`, which imports the JavaScript/TypeScript `security-and-quality` suite and excludes only `js/http-to-file-access`.
Pointed the CodeQL workflow at the checked-in maintained-source suite while keeping the existing generated-output `paths-ignore` config.
Documented that the excluded HTTP-to-file findings are expected authenticated upload persistence or local smoke/proof artifact writes to fixed/generated paths.
Normalized upload payloads to a Buffer copy before local or S3 persistence.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.34
releases.summary: This patch continues maintained-source CodeQL cleanup by making the existing custom CSRF middleware recognizable to CodeQL while preserving the browser cookie-session CSRF contract.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Kept mutating browser requests on the existing double-submit CSRF cookie and `x-csrf-token` header validation path.
Made the default `csrf_token` cookie read explicit when the deployment uses the default CSRF cookie name, while preserving custom `CSRF_COOKIE_NAME` support.
Added unit coverage for accepted matching CSRF tokens and rejected missing CSRF headers.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.33
releases.summary: This patch continues maintained-source CodeQL cleanup by reshaping platform registration so invite lookup, public self-registration, and failure metrics are separated into explicit server-policy decisions.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Normalized platform invite tokens before lookup and always ran the bounded invite lookup for platform registration.
Kept invite-based onboarding, first-user bootstrap, SMTP-backed public self-registration, and homelab local-account registration behavior intact.
Centralized registration failure recording so user-provided invite-token presence no longer guards the auth metrics action.
Updated authentication documentation to reflect the current configurable platform self-registration behavior.
